discovery.run_summary: Risk-off in tech (XLK -1.85%, SMH -1.2%, AAPL -3.6% on Siri reveal, ORCL -2.8%) with broad rotation into defensives, financials, real estate, and small-caps (IWM +0.32% vs QQQ -1.15%). VIX up 5% to 19.87 with intraday spike to 23.34 suggests volatility event mid-day. Mid-cap movers skew toward housing/construction (POOL, BLDR), airlines (ALK, SKYW), and selective semicap (VECO) bouncing against the tape.

notable_index_moves: QQQ -1.15% vs IWM +0.32% — clear rotation out of mega-cap tech into small/mid-caps
XLRE +2.13% and XLU +1.06% — defensive bid suggests rate expectations softening ahead of CPI tomorrow
VIX intraday range 17.52-23.34 — sharp vol spike that compressed by close
AAPL -3.64% on Siri/WWDC reveal disappointment dragging XLK

discoveries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semicap equipment name spiking against a red SMH tape with no visible catalyst
discoveries.thesis: VECO ripping ~10% on ~3x volume while SMH is down 1.2% is highly unusual and suggests a company-specific catalyst (analyst upgrade, contract win, M&A chatter, or industry conference comments) not visible in our 8-K/news feed. Without identifiable catalyst, direction of fade vs. follow-through cannot be called with conf 3+.
discoveries.what_confirms: Discovery of a specific catalyst (HBM/advanced packaging contract, broker upgrade with raised PT, M&A rumor) would convert this to UNDERDONE if sustainable, OVERDONE if rumor-only
discoveries.what_kills: If the move is pure short-squeeze with no fundamental driver, fade likely; if a major contract is confirmed (e.g., advanced packaging for HBM), continuation likely
discoveries.what_to_learn: Single-name semicap outperformance on red SMH days almost always has a specific named catalyst — worth tracking down before classifying
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no news in feed
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals dict, no relevant news items
research_pointers: Check for any analyst actions on VECO today
Scan for advanced packaging / HBM equipment contract announcements
Check options flow for unusual call activity suggesting M&A speculation
Review peer ASML/AMAT/LRCX commentary at recent investor conferences

discoveries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Airline rally on the heels of an officer-change 8-K with FD disclosure
discoveries.thesis: ALK up ~7% on ~1.8x volume following a 5.02 + 7.01 + 9.01 8-K from June 3. The combination of officer change PLUS Reg FD disclosure suggests management transition was accompanied by some forward-looking statement (possibly raised guidance or strategic update). Can't determine direction of news from item codes alone, and SKYW also up 5% suggests sector tailwind component. Sector strength + ambiguous filing = direction not callable at conf 3+.
discoveries.what_confirms: If 8-K exhibits show raised guidance or new strategic plan, UNDERDONE thesis emerges; if it's just a routine officer transition with sector lifting all boats, RATIONAL is correct
discoveries.what_kills: If SKYW and other airlines are rallying equally on jet fuel/oil weakness (XLE -1.61%), this is sector sympathy not company-specific
discoveries.catalyst: 5.02 officer departure/appointment + 7.01 Reg FD disclosure June 3; possibly amplified by lower oil
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K item 7.01 (Reg FD) typically contains substantive forward-looking disclosure; pairing with 5.02 is notable
research_pointers: Read the June 3 8-K exhibit to determine if Reg FD disclosure includes guidance
Compare ALK move to peers (UAL, DAL, LUV) — is this airline-wide or ALK-specific?
Check crude oil action today as airline cost tailwind

discoveries.name: Pool Corporation
discoveries.setup: Housing-adjacent distributor rally with no visible catalyst, coinciding with XLRE strength
discoveries.thesis: POOL up ~6% on 1.7x volume with no 8-K and no news. Likely tied to the rotation into housing/rate-sensitive names (XLRE +2.13%) on softer rate expectations into tomorrow's CPI. BLDR also up 6% supports housing-rotation thesis. This is more of a thematic move than a stock-specific mispricing — UNCLEAR because magnitude (6%+) seems large for pure rotation absent a specific catalyst.
discoveries.what_confirms: If broader housing complex (LEN, DHI, NVR, HD, LOW) is also up materially, this is RATIONAL sector rotation; cool CPI tomorrow would extend the move
discoveries.what_kills: Hot CPI print tomorrow would unwind the rate-sensitive rotation and these names likely give back gains
discoveries.what_to_learn: Pre-CPI rate-sensitive rotation can drive 5-7% single-day moves in housing-complex mid-caps without any company catalyst — worth tagging as positioning, not signal
discoveries.catalyst: Apparent rotation into rate-sensitive / housing complex ahead of CPI
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals, but coincides with XLRE +2.13% and BLDR co-move
research_pointers: Check homebuilder ETFs (XHB, ITB) for sector confirmation
Position size relative to CPI binary tomorrow — this is a CPI-trade exposure
Review POOL's recent estimates and how a housing recovery thesis is being priced

discoveries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Building products name rallying on officer-change 8-K combined with rate-sensitive rotation
discoveries.thesis: BLDR up 6% on only 1.1x volume — note the lighter relative volume is notable. The 8-K is a single 5.02 item (officer change), which is rarely a 6% catalyst on its own. The move is more likely 80% sector rotation (housing/rates pre-CPI) + 20% reaction to whatever the 5.02 says. If CPI runs hot tomorrow, the rotation component reverses sharply. Fade setup into binary catalyst with stretched positioning.
discoveries.what_confirms: Hot CPI print Wednesday triggering reversal in rate-sensitive complex; light volume on the rally suggests limited conviction
discoveries.what_kills: Cool CPI extending rotation; or 8-K reveals a positive strategic appointment (new CEO with strong track record); or homebuilder peers continue to outperform
discoveries.what_to_learn: Solo 5.02 8-Ks rarely justify 6%+ moves on their own — when paired with concurrent sector rotation, the sector component dominates and is mean-reverting to the macro catalyst (CPI here)
discoveries.catalyst: 5.02 officer change 8-K June 5 + housing/rate-sensitive sector rotation ahead of CPI
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Single-item 5.02 8-K is a modest catalyst; light 1.1x volume on a 6% move suggests sector flow, not conviction buying
research_pointers: Read 8-K to identify who is being appointed/departing
Size position with CPI tomorrow as a binary
Cross-check with homebuilder peers — if BLDR is ahead of LEN/DHI/PHM, the alpha is BLDR-specific; if in-line, it's purely rotation

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Defense drone name selling off sharply against a green ITA (defense) sector tape
discoveries.thesis: AVAV down 4.4% today and 13.6% over 5 days while ITA is up 1.4% and other defense names like DCO are up 4%. This is significant divergence from a strong sector. No 8-K, no news in feed — suggests possible secondary offering, contract loss, broker downgrade, or earnings positioning. Without catalyst visibility, cannot assess if oversold (UNDERDONE bounce) or just-starting (continuation down).
discoveries.what_confirms: Discovery of a downgrade, lost contract, or pending offering would justify the divergence; if none exists, the divergence is overdone and reversal likely
discoveries.what_kills: If AVAV reports earnings soon and is being de-risked into print, no mispricing — just positioning
discoveries.what_to_learn: Persistent multi-day divergence from a strong sector (-13% in 5 days vs +1.4% sector today) almost always has a specific catalyst — worth running it down before trading either direction
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; sector divergence is the signal itself
research_pointers: Check AVAV earnings calendar — is a print imminent?
Scan for recent broker downgrades or short reports
Check for secondary offering announcements
Compare to drone peers (KTOS, RKLB)

discoveries.name: Masimo Corporation
discoveries.setup: Flat price action on 3.1x volume — unusual volume without price translation
discoveries.thesis: MASI closed essentially unchanged (+0.01%) on 3.14x normal volume. High volume + no price move = institutional rotation, block crossing, or a balanced fight between bulls and bears on a specific catalyst we can't see. This pattern often precedes a directional break once one side wins. No 8-K visible, no news. Worth flagging as something is happening here.
discoveries.what_confirms: A subsequent directional break (up or down) on similar volume would reveal which side won; news of a strategic review, activist position, or M&A would explain the absorption
discoveries.what_kills: If volume returns to normal and price stays range-bound, this was just an isolated rebalance flow with no signal
discoveries.what_to_learn: Flat price + 3x volume is one of the highest signal-to-noise patterns — institutional accumulation/distribution leaves this fingerprint before the move is visible in price
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — possibly institutional repositioning or M&A-related
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 3.14x volume with 0% price change is the signature of large two-sided flow
research_pointers: Check 13D/13G filings on MASI for activist activity (Politan has prior history here)
Monitor for follow-through directional move in next 1-3 sessions
Check options flow for unusual large block trades

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Pre-CPI rotation into rate-sensitive complex (XLRE +2.13%, XLU +1.06%, IWM > QQQ)
Apple Siri AI reveal disappoints, dragging XLK -1.85% and AAPL -3.64%

discovery.no_signals_note: CPI print Wednesday morning is the dominant binary — many of today's moves (POOL, BLDR, XLRE/XLU outperformance) are CPI-positioning trades and should be treated as exposure to that print, not standalone theses. Tech weakness driven by Apple/WWDC reaction. VIX intraday spike to 23.34 then close at 19.87 suggests something happened mid-day worth investigating in tape (possibly Iran/Hormuz headline at 18:46 UTC).

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic releases Claude Fable 5 (Mythos-class), including vibe-coding/game generation
ai_announcements.summary: Anthropic's first publicly available Mythos-class model, with strong code/game generation capability. Released alongside news that Lovable hit $500M ARR with 1M projects/week, validating the vibe-coding category.
ai_announcements.no_signals_note: None of the moving stocks in today's market data have plausible direct linkage to the Fable 5 / vibe-coding news. Veeco (semicap), airlines (ALK/SKYW), POOL, BLDR, AXSM (biotech), MASI, FIZZ, DCO, AVAV, VCYT, AMR are all moving on non-AI catalysts (sector rotation, 8-Ks, defense/coal commodity moves).

ai_announcements.headline: Apple WWDC: revamped Siri, Apple Intelligence improvements, iOS 27
ai_announcements.summary: Apple positions AI as one piece of broader software improvements; demos appeared more grounded post-$250M false ad settlement. Narrative: 'slow-and-steady' Apple AI bet looking smarter.
ai_announcements.no_signals_note: No Apple-supply-chain or Apple-competitor names in the moving list today. MASI (formerly an Apple legal adversary) is flat with elevated volume but no directional move.

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI files confidentially for IPO (following Anthropic)
ai_announcements.summary: Both frontier labs now on IPO path; 'MANGOS' narrative replacing FAANG. Strategic for private market valuations and AI capex narrative but no direct read-through to today's movers.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Today's biggest movers (VECO +10%, ALK +6.8%, POOL +6.3%, BLDR +6%, AVAV -4.4%, AMR -4.3%) do not map to any of today's AI announcements. VECO move is semicap-specific (likely WFE/HBM read-through, not Fable 5 related). Airlines and POOL/BLDR look like a consumer/cyclical risk-on rotation. AVAV continues post-earnings drift (-13.6% 5d) unrelated to AI news. No actionable AI-overreaction trades identified in this dataset.
